mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month. This mechanism is always active as retail sales data is a leading indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change versus S&P 500 forward 20-day return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the spread between top and bottom quintile RSAFS growth portfolios is not positive and significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in the federal funds rate, specifically a monthly increase exceeding 25 basis points, signals that the Federal Reserve is tightening more aggressively than expected. This compresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: In all instances where FEDFUNDS monthly change exceeds 0.25%, the average S&P 500 forward 20-day return is not negative, or more than 50% of such episodes produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) jointly reflect the health of the manufacturing and labor sectors. When both are rising month-over-month, the economy is expanding consistently, supporting corporate profits and boosting S&P 500 forward returns. The signal requires both proxies to confirm a synchronized expansion.
failure_condition: The average S&P 500 forward 20-day return following months where both INDPRO and PAYEMS increase is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the signal yields a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is already below 5% (indicative of a tight labor market), a further decline of 0.2 percentage points or more in a month signals overheating that will force the Fed to tighten policy. This leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days. The mechanism is regime-conditional, only active when UNRATE is low.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by at least 0.2% in a month
failure_condition: For months where UNRATE is below 5% and declines by >= 0.2%, the median S&P 500 forward 20-day return is not negative, or the hit rate (fraction of negative returns) does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer confidence. A sharp month-over-month decline exceeding 5% signals a loss of momentum in the housing market, which spills over into broader economic weakness and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The average S&P 500 forward 60-day return following HOUST declines >5% is not negative, or the strategy fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) shows a month-over-month increase above 0.2% while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals that real interest rates are becoming negative and the Fed may be forced to raise rates to contain inflation. This expectation of tightening leads to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. The interaction between rising inflation and low policy rates is critical.
failure_condition: In instances where CPIAUCSL rises >0.2% MoM and FEDFUNDS <=2%, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative on average, or the signal's information coefficient (IC) is less than -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a coincident indicator of manufacturing output. A positive month-over-month change in INDPRO signals expanding production capacity and economic strength, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the following week (5 trading days).
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5-day S&P 500 returns is not positive (Pearson r <= 0) over any full economic cycle, or the rank IC averaged over 1-year windows is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with robust payroll expansion (PAYEMS) provides a comprehensive measure of aggregate demand. When both are above their respective trailing 12-month moving averages, the economy is experiencing broad-based strength. This signals sustainable corporate profit growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly (60 trading day) horizon.
failure_condition: The average S&P 500 forward 60-day return when both GDPC1 and PAYEMS are above their 12-month moving averages is not higher than the unconditional average, or the signal's Sharpe ratio over any 10-year period is below 0.3.
